ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.quality_notes: Text-based PDF with clear fields. Certificate page 1 is the COO. Page 2 is an embedded commercial invoice (CGC0039-2) attached to the certificate file.

invoice.quality_notes: Text-based PDF. Invoice Number INV-000050, issued by mFiber AB, billed to Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India. This is a DIFFERENT invoice from the one referenced in the certificate (CGC0039-2).

document_pairing.method: Rule 8.5 – File-based pairing. Certificate from file 1 (page 1), invoice from file 2 (page 1).
document_pairing.pairing_notes: Certificate references 'Ref no: CGC0039-2'. The embedded invoice on page 2 of the certificate file also bears reference CGC0039-2. However, per rule 8.5.0, the invoice for verification must come from the SEPARATE invoice file (Document B). Document B contains invoice INV-000050, which does NOT bear the reference CGC0039-2. Per rule 8.5.2, when the certificate references a specific invoice number/reference but no invoice in the invoice file bears that reference, the result must be MANUAL_REVIEW. Additionally, pairing validation per 8.6 is performed below.

pairing_validation_8_6.criterion_1_detail: Certificate references CGC0039-2. Invoice file contains INV-000050. No match.
pairing_validation_8_6.criterion_2_detail: Certificate consignor 'mFiber AB' can be identified in invoice file (Document B) in footer: 'mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden' and in header branding 'mFiber'.
pairing_validation_8_6.criterion_3_detail: Certificate consignee 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD' does NOT appear anywhere in invoice file (Document B). Invoice is billed to 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd'.
pairing_validation_8_6.validation_result: PASS (criterion 2 met – consignor identified in invoice). However, rule 8.5.2 takes precedence: the certificate references a specific invoice (CGC0039-2) that cannot be found in the invoice file.

lc_check.lc_verification_steps: No LC-related terminology found in certificate. References 'CGC0039-2' and 'SEG-25D-366076' are document/certificate numbers, not LC references.

search_results.location: Document B (invoice file), page 1, header branding
search_results.notes: Logo/branding text 'mFiber' at top of invoice page. Also 'mfiber AB' in footer.

search_results.location: Document B (invoice file), page 1, footer address block
search_results.text_found: mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden
search_results.notes: Full company name and address in footer of invoice.

search_results.location: Document B (invoice file), page 1, authorized signature area
search_results.notes: Company stamp area near authorized signature.

consignor_search_step_2b.conclusion: Consignor 'mFiber AB' can be identified in the invoice file (Document B) as the invoice issuer.

rule_activation.notes: Rule 8.5.2 is triggered: certificate references CGC0039-2 but invoice file contains INV-000050. The referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file. This is a critical blocking finding.

consignor.certificate_value: mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14, Gothenburg, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden (footer/issuer)

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found in invoice file
Land verification via VAT prefix SE in SE556863963601

comparison_steps.detail: mFiber AB found in Document B footer as 'mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden'. Also found in header branding as 'mFiber'. Also found near authorized signature stamp.
Certificate consignor 'mFiber AB' expressly appears in the invoice as the invoice issuer (footer address block and header branding). This satisfies rule 4.1.0.
Certificate: Sweden. Invoice footer: Sweden. Also VAT no SE556863963601 → SE prefix confirms Sweden.

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor 'mFiber AB' with address 'Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14, Gothenburg, Sweden' is identical to the invoice issuer shown in the footer of Document B: 'mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden'. Name match is exact after normalization. Country Sweden confirmed via explicit address and VAT prefix SE. Rule 4.1.0 applies – consignor name expressly found in invoice.
consignor.motivation: Consignor 'mFiber AB' in certificate matches the invoice issuer 'mFiber AB' in Document B exactly. Address and country (Sweden) also match. MATCH via rule 4.1.0.

consignee.certificate_value: CENTURY GATE CO. LTD, P.O. BOX 14368, C R NO. 1010277465, RIYADH 7097, Saudi Arabia
consignee.invoice_value: Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, 403, 4th Floor, Orion Business Park, Thane West, 400607 Maharashtra, India

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification – primary billing field checked first
rules_applied.result: Bill To field contains 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd' – not CENTURY GATE CO. LTD

rules_applied.description: Search in all relevant invoice fields
rules_applied.result: CENTURY GATE CO. LTD not found anywhere in Document B

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – search in all fields including delivery, importer, dealer
rules_applied.result: CENTURY GATE CO. LTD not found

comparison_steps.detail: Invoice Bill To: 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India'. Certificate consignee: 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD, Saudi Arabia'. These are completely different companies in different countries.
Searched Ship To (Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India), all address blocks, all text in Document B. 'CENTURY GATE' does not appear anywhere.
Certificate consignee country: Saudi Arabia. Invoice Bill To country: India. Country mismatch.

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD' in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia cannot be found anywhere in Document B (invoice file). The invoice is billed to and shipped to 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd' in India – a completely different company in a completely different country. No trace of 'CENTURY GATE' exists in the invoice file. This is a clear MISMATCH both on company name and country.
consignee.motivation: Certificate consignee 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD, Saudi Arabia' cannot be identified anywhere in the invoice. The invoice is addressed to 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India' – a different company in a different country. MISMATCH per rules 4.2.1 and 4.2.4.

goods_description.certificate_value: PAPER IN REELS, 434 reels of Tissue Paper - (Napkin & Facial)

rules_applied.description: Controlled text matching – identity-bearing main designation
rules_applied.result: MISMATCH – 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' vs 'Kraft Liner Board' are entirely different products

comparison_steps.detail: No article numbers in certificate. Proceed to Priority 2.

Certificate: 'Tissue Paper - (Napkin & Facial)'. Invoice: 'Kraft Liner Board'. These are completely different paper products. 'Tissue Paper' is a soft hygiene product; 'Kraft Liner Board' is a heavy packaging material. No word overlap in the identity-bearing designations. No normalization can reconcile these.

Certificate also states 'PAPER IN REELS'. Invoice describes 'Kraft Liner Board' in reels. Both are paper products in reels, but the specific product type is completely different. The general term 'PAPER IN REELS' does not satisfy verification since the certificate also provides a specific designation 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' which must be verifiable.

goods_description.debug_explanation: The certificate describes '434 reels of Tissue Paper - (Napkin & Facial)' as the goods. The invoice (Document B) describes 'Kraft Liner Board, HS Code: 4804 41 00'. These are fundamentally different paper products. Tissue Paper (napkin & facial) is a lightweight hygiene product. Kraft Liner Board is a heavy-duty packaging material. The HS code in the invoice (4804 41) also differs from what would be expected for tissue paper. No amount of normalization can make these the same product. Clear MISMATCH.
goods_description.motivation: Certificate describes 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' while invoice describes 'Kraft Liner Board'. These are entirely different products. MISMATCH per rule 4.3.3.

rules_applied.description: Exception 3B – MT to KG conversion: 53.89 MT = 53890 kg ≠ 88452 kg
rules_applied.result: MISMATCH – values do not match even after conversion

comparison_steps.detail: Certificate states GW/NW: 88452 kg. Invoice total: 53.8900 MT. 88452 does not appear in invoice. 53.89 MT = 53890 kg ≠ 88452 kg. Also 434 reels (cert) vs 18 reels (invoice). Both numeric values are completely different.
53.8900 MT × 1000 = 53890 kg. Certificate states 88452 kg. Difference: 34562 kg (64.1% deviation). Far exceeds ±0.1% tolerance.
Certificate: 434 reels. Invoice: 4 + 14 = 18 units (reels). Completely different quantities.

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate states GW/NW: 88452 kg for 434 reels of Tissue Paper. Invoice states Total MT: 53.8900 (= 53890 kg) for 18 reels of Kraft Liner Board. The weight difference is 34562 kg (64% deviation), far exceeding the ±0.1% tolerance. The reel count (434 vs 18) also differs massively. This confirms these are different shipments entirely. Clear MISMATCH.
quantity.motivation: Certificate states 88452 kg (434 reels). Invoice states 53.89 MT = 53890 kg (18 reels). Both weight and reel count differ significantly. MISMATCH per rule 4.4.1.

rules_applied.result: Invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Certificate states 'India'. These do not match.

comparison_steps.detail: Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin: Sweden' in a dedicated field. Certificate states origin 'India' in box 3.
No normalization can reconcile 'India' and 'Sweden'. These are completely different countries. Not EU-equivalent, not covered by any special normalization rule.
Certificate origin 'India' cannot be identified in invoice. Invoice states 'Sweden'. Direct contradiction.

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states Country of Origin: India. The invoice (Document B) has an explicit 'Country of Origin' field stating 'Sweden'. India ≠ Sweden. These are entirely different countries with no normalization path. This is a direct contradiction. Clear MISMATCH.
country_of_origin.motivation: Certificate states origin 'India'. Invoice states origin 'Sweden'. Direct contradiction. MISMATCH per rule 4.5.5.

evidence.description: Country of Origin in invoice file

rule_evaluation_log.result: Certificate from Document A page 1, invoice from Document B page 1. Document A page 2 contains an embedded invoice (CGC0039-2) but per rule 8.5.0 this is NOT the invoice for verification.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Certificate references specific invoice (CGC0039-2) but invoice file contains INV-000050
rule_evaluation_log.result: TRIGGERED – The referenced invoice CGC0039-2 cannot be found in the invoice file (Document B). Per rule 8.5.2, result should be MANUAL_REVIEW. However, continuing full verification reveals multiple MISMATCH findings (consignee, goods, quantity, origin) confirming these are entirely different transactions. NOT_IDENTICAL is the appropriate final result.

Criterion 1 (invoice ref): FAIL – CGC0039-2 ≠ INV-000050. Criterion 2 (consignor): PASS – mFiber AB found in both. Criterion 3 (consignee): FAIL – CENTURY GATE CO. LTD not in invoice. At least one criterion met (criterion 2), so pairing validation passes technically, but 8.5.2 still triggered.
No LC reference found. Not activated.
mFiber AB found in invoice file footer and header. MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Country verification via indirect identifiers (VAT prefix)
rule_evaluation_log.result: VAT no SE556863963601 → SE prefix confirms Sweden. MATCH.
Bill To field: Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India. Not CENTURY GATE CO. LTD.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Search consignee in all invoice fields
rule_evaluation_log.result: CENTURY GATE CO. LTD not found anywhere in Document B.
MISMATCH – 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' ≠ 'Kraft Liner Board'. Entirely different products.
88452 kg not found in invoice. 53890 kg (from 53.89 MT) ≠ 88452 kg. MISMATCH.
Invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Certificate states 'India'. MISMATCH.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: The embedded invoice on page 2 of the certificate file (CGC0039-2) should be used as the verification invoice.
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Rule 8.5.0 explicitly prohibits using an embedded invoice from the certificate file for verification. The invoice for verification must come from the separate invoice file (Document B). Using the embedded copy would mean verifying the certificate against itself.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Since the consignor (mFiber AB) matches, perhaps the wrong invoice was attached and the result should be MANUAL_REVIEW rather than NOT_IDENTICAL.
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: While rule 8.5.2 would normally suggest MANUAL_REVIEW when the referenced invoice cannot be found in the invoice file, full verification reveals that FOUR out of FIVE checkpoints are MISMATCH (consignee, goods, quantity, origin). This is not a borderline case requiring human review – the documents clearly describe entirely different transactions. Per rule 6.1, NOT_IDENTICAL is appropriate when at least one checkpoint is MISMATCH. With four MISMATCH results, the conclusion is unambiguous.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Both documents involve paper products, so goods description could be considered a match at a general level.
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Rule 4.3.3 requires the identity-bearing main designation to be identifiable. 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' and 'Kraft Liner Board' are fundamentally different products despite both being paper. No normalization rule permits equating these.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Certifikatet avser en leverans av 434 rullar Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial) till CENTURY GATE CO. LTD i Saudiarabien med ursprung Indien, medan den bifogade fakturan (INV-000050) avser en leverans av Kraft Liner Board till Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd i Indien med ursprung Sverige. Mottagare, varubeskrivning, kvantitet och ursprungsland skiljer sig helt mellan dokumenten. Det verkar som att fel faktura har bifogats till certifikatet.
blocking_findings: Consignee MISMATCH: Certificate states 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD, Saudi Arabia' but invoice is billed to 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, India'.
Goods MISMATCH: Certificate states 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' but invoice describes 'Kraft Liner Board'.
Quantity MISMATCH: Certificate states 88452 kg / 434 reels but invoice states 53.89 MT / 18 reels.
Origin MISMATCH: Certificate states 'India' but invoice states 'Sweden'.
Invoice reference mismatch: Certificate references CGC0039-2 but invoice file contains INV-000050.
overall_assessment.summary: The certificate and the attached invoice (Document B) describe entirely different commercial transactions. The certificate references invoice CGC0039-2 for a shipment of Tissue Paper to Saudi Arabia with Indian origin, while the invoice file contains INV-000050 for Kraft Liner Board to India with Swedish origin. Only the consignor (mFiber AB) is common between the two documents. Four of five checkpoints are MISMATCH. It appears the wrong invoice was attached.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot faktura
human_readable_report.invoice_number: INV-000050 (from invoice file; certificate references CGC0039-2)

sections.certificate_value: mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14, Gothenburg, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: mFiber AB, Drottninggatan 21, SE-411 14 Gothenburg, Sweden (issuer/footer)
sections.rule_applied: Koncernstruktur (4.1.0) – consignor name found in invoice issuer. Land via address and VAT prefix SE.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets consignor 'mFiber AB' med adress i Gothenburg, Sweden är identisk med fakturans utställare som framgår i fakturans sidfot och header. Namn och land (Sverige) matchar exakt.

sections.certificate_value: CENTURY GATE CO. LTD, P.O. BOX 14368, C R NO. 1010277465, RIYADH 7097, Saudi Arabia
sections.invoice_value: Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd, 403, 4th Floor, Orion Business Park, Thane West, 400607 Maharashtra, India
sections.rule_applied: Consignee-identifiering (4.2.0.2, 4.2.1, 4.2.4) – certifikatets mottagare kan inte identifieras i fakturan.
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'CENTURY GATE CO. LTD' i Saudiarabien förekommer inte någonstans i fakturan. Fakturan är ställd till 'Paper Vantage Point Pvt Ltd' i Indien — ett helt annat företag i ett helt annat land. MISMATCH.

sections.certificate_value: PAPER IN REELS, 434 reels of Tissue Paper - (Napkin & Facial)
sections.rule_applied: Identitetsbärande huvudbeteckning (4.3.3) – 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' ≠ 'Kraft Liner Board'.
sections.motivation: Certifikatet beskriver 'Tissue Paper (Napkin & Facial)' medan fakturan beskriver 'Kraft Liner Board'. Dessa är helt olika pappersprodukter och ingen normalisering kan likställa dem. MISMATCH.
Certifikatet anger GW/NW: 88452 kg och 434 rullar. Fakturan anger 53.89 MT = 53890 kg och 18 rullar. Både vikt och antal skiljer sig drastiskt. MISMATCH.
Certifikatet anger ursprungsland 'India'. Fakturan anger 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Dessa är helt olika länder. MISMATCH.

human_readable_report.overall_explanation: Fyra av fem kontrollpunkter ger MISMATCH. Certifikatet och den bifogade fakturan avser helt olika transaktioner. Certifikatet refererar till faktura CGC0039-2 men den bifogade fakturafilen innehåller INV-000050. Det verkar som att fel faktura har bifogats.

output_quality_checks.consistency_check_detail: 4 MISMATCH checkpoints found (consignee, goods, quantity, origin). comparison_result = NOT_IDENTICAL. Consistent. No motivations contradict their status values.
